panels.description: Wide shot: A tall schooner and a small coracle are being violently swept by a churning, bubbling current through narrow straits towards the open sea. The water is chaotic and white-capped. The schooner is shown listing sharply, suggesting imminent danger, with faint figures running on deck. Dramatic lighting emphasizing the turbulent water.

Close up on the interior of the small coracle. Jim (implied narrator) is lying flat in the bottom, face pale with sheer terror, eyes wide, clutching the sides of the boat. The perspective should be low, looking up slightly at the churning grey water visible just over the rim of the boat. Heavy, claustrophobic shadows.

bubbles.text: I lay down flat in the bottom of that wretched skiff and devoutly recommended my spirit to its Maker.

panels.description: Medium shot: The coracle is tossed high on a massive, dark wave, silhouetted against the raging, foam-crested water ahead, suggesting the 'bar of raging breakers.' Jim's hand is visible gripping the edge of the boat, knuckles white. The atmosphere is oppressive and dark, focusing on the immediate threat of the wave crash.

Medium close up: Jim's face, now slightly less panicked, showing deep exhaustion. His expression is slackening as sleep takes hold amidst the chaos of the tossing boat. Water spray softly catches the light, creating a dreamlike filter over the scene. Faint, soft lighting contrasting with the previous panels.

Wide shot: Bright morning light illuminates the scene. Jim is waking up in the coracle, which is bobbing gently in calmer water. In the background, the massive, green-sloped Spy-glass hill dominates the horizon, suggesting landfall at Treasure Island. Calm, clear sky.

Medium shot: View from the coracle looking towards Haulbowline Head. Steep, dark, rocky cliffs rise abruptly from the sea, covered in fallen debris. Jim's face is seen in profile, showing slight apprehension as he considers landing near the imposing cliffs.

bubbles.text: I was scarce a quarter of a mile to seaward, and it was my first thought to paddle in and land.

panels.description: Wide action shot: The water near the base of the rocks is filled with large, dark, glistening shapes—the sea lions—some barking and splashing noisily as they move on the rocks. Jim's coracle is seen pulling away slightly from the shore in the foreground, his expression showing clear disgust and fear.

bubbles.text: The look of them... was more than enough to disgust me of that landing-place!
